description: Claude receives partially-filled template + project context → generate %%POSTER_BODY%% content

project_context.objective: Apply IQA energy decomposition to train ML models on QM40 molecular dataset
key_results: IQA atomic energies as ML targets outperform global energy (R²=0.97 vs 0.91)

expected_behavior: Generates 2-column portrait body with: Left col = Introduction (IQA motivation, QCT background, objective), Methods (AIMALL, DFT level, dataset). Right col = Results (R²=0.97, figure placeholder for learning curves), Key Result alertblock (R²=0.97 vs 0.91), Conclusions (3-4 bullets), References (\printbibliography). LaTeX is syntactically valid. Figure placeholders use %% INSERT FIGURE format.

description: --empty flag: fill_template.py runs, then Claude writes TODO skeleton for %%POSTER_BODY%%
expected_behavior: Claude generates %%POSTER_BODY%% as structural skeleton with %% TODO: placeholders only. No project context loaded, no real content generated. Writes main.tex and support files. Reports list of %% TODO: markers by section name. Does NOT generate abstract or Q&A.

description: --abstract flag: generate 300-400 word submission abstract from project context

project_context.objective: Apply IQA energy decomposition to train ML models predicting atomic and interaction energies
key_results: Atomic IQA energies: R²=0.97 on test set
Interaction IQA terms (Vcl, Vxc) learnable independently
Model generalises across functional groups in QM40

expected_behavior: Produces 300-400 word abstract. Opens with scientific problem (ML + QCT/IQA gap). States methods (IQA, QM40, model types). Reports key result (R²=0.97, atomic energies). Closes with conclusion and outlook. Followed by 5-8 keywords. Written to abstract.md. Printed inline in report.
